meta.title: Convert MOV to MP3 — Free, In Your Browser, No Upload | {{SITE_NAME}}
meta.description: Extract the audio from a .mov video to an .mp3 file right in your browser. Nothing is uploaded — your file never leaves your device. Free, no sign-up, no watermark.
og.title: Convert MOV to MP3 — Free, No Upload
og.description: Pull the audio out of a .mov (QuickTime) video into .mp3, locally in your browser.
hero.title: Convert MOV to MP3 — right in your browser
hero.lead: Drop a .mov video — an iPhone clip, a screen recording, an interview — and pull out just the audio as an .mp3. It's extracted on your own device, so the file is never uploaded.
prose.p1: MOV is the QuickTime video format your iPhone and Mac record in. When you only need the sound — the dialogue from a clip, the music from a recording, the audio of a lecture or interview — extracting to MP3 drops the video entirely and leaves a small file that plays in any music app, car stereo or podcast player.
prose.p2: Most converters upload your whole video to a server first, which is slow for large .mov files and means handing over your footage. {{SITE_NAME}} doesn't — the audio is extracted locally in your browser, so nothing is stored or logged and even a big recording starts instantly with no upload.
faq.a1: No. The extraction happens entirely in your browser, on your own device. There's no server that could receive the file — open your browser's Network tab while it runs and you'll see zero bytes leave.
faq.q2: What MP3 quality do I get?
faq.a2: You can pick the bitrate before converting — from 128 kbps (small) up to 320 kbps (best quality). 192 kbps is a good default for speech and most music.
faq.q3: Can it handle a long or large MOV?
faq.a3: Yes. Audio is tiny next to video, so even a long .mov extracts quickly and the resulting MP3 stays small — and because nothing uploads, file size isn't limited by a server.
